ksa_text: Skill in writing and refactoring ML code to production quality, including data processing, feature engineering, model training, evaluation pipelines, documentation, unit testing, and the transformation of research-quality prototypes into deployable systems.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Organizational teams are committed to a culture that considers and communicates AI risk.

top_anchors.element_label: Organizational teams are committed to a culture that considers and communicates AI risk.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: This skill KSA describes execution-level work in AI/ML Engineering that is not expected of a Chief of Staff to CDAIO (senior specialist tier). The role is responsible for overseeing and directing this work, not performing it directly. Excluded on execution-seniority grounds.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could this execution-level skill KSA be expected of a Chief of Staff to CDAIO in real-world practice?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Compensation benchmarks and leadership effectiveness research confirm that senior specialist-tier roles direct and oversee execution work in AI/ML Engineering rather than performing it. Role scope analysis supports exclusion.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: execution-level skill KSA in AI/ML Engineering is appropriately excluded for a non-execution senior specialist role. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Execution-level skill KSA in AI/ML Engineering is outside the expected competency scope for a Chief of Staff to CDAIO (senior specialist tier). The role oversees this work; it does not perform it directly.
